generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Advanced LiDAR sensor for 3D environmental mapping and object detection in autonomous vehicle applications. Operates at high frequency (≥10Hz) to provide real-time point cloud data for perception modules."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance RadarSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Radar sensor for velocity and distance measurement of surrounding objects, complementing LiDAR data for robust perception in varying weather conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RadarObjectData
]

instance CameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution camera system for visual perception, providing RGB images and semantic segmentation data for object detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraImageData
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensor monitoring motor actuation state, including RPM, torque, and power consumption for vehicle dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateData
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor tracking steering servo position and angle, providing real-time feedback for trajectory control and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive sensor suite capturing vehicle dynamics (speed, acceleration, braking status) and environmental conditions for ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw and clustered LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from the Physical Twin Entity to the Digital Twin Entity via UDP over Wi-Fi. Includes 3D spatial coordinates and reflectance values."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance RadarObjectData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed radar data containing object velocity, distance, and classification transmitted for sensor fusion in the Digital Twin Entity."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RadarSensor
]

instance CameraImageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "RGB images and semantic segmentation masks from camera sensors, transmitted for object detection and environmental context analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CameraSensor
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "High-frequency motor state data (≥100Hz) including RPM, torque, and power consumption for real-time vehicle dynamics monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering servo position and angle data transmitted for trajectory control and path planning validation in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, braking status, and environmental conditions for ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks derived from LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking, representing detected vehicles, obstacles, and lane boundaries."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor, CameraSensor, RadarSensor
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPath : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Derived driving path data from sensor fusion and vehicle state analysis, representing the planned trajectory for ACC functionality."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor, ObjectTracksData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model for clustering raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks using DBSCAN or similar algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler
]

instance ObjectDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "YOLO or PointPillars-based model for detecting and classifying objects in LiDAR and camera data streams."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerceptionEnabler
]

instance MpcControllerModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model Predictive Control (MPC) algorithm for trajectory optimization and adaptive speed control in ACC applications."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point cloud data after noise filtering, coordinate transformation, and occlusion handling for visualization and further processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks with metadata (ID, velocity, bounding box) derived from sensor fusion and clustering algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle state data with standardized units and derived metrics (e.g., acceleration trends) for ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData, MotorStateData, SteeringServoData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler, DataStorageEnabler
]

instance ProjectedPathProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Optimized driving path with trajectory points and safety margins derived from MPC and sensor fusion."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedDrivingPath
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler, ControlLogicEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudClusteringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based software component for clustering raw LiDAR point clouds into object tracks using DBSCAN or similar algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance PerceptionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Sensor fusion and object detection pipeline using YOLO/PointPillars models to process LiDAR, radar, and camera data into structured object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud/edge-hosted ACC logic execution environment running MPC and PID controllers for adaptive speed control and emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Web-based 3D visualization framework (React/Angular) for real-time rendering of vehicle state, object tracks, and projected paths."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database (InfluxDB/TimescaleDB) for storing vehicle state, sensor logs, and processed data with high write throughput."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "5G/edge-based middleware for low-latency UDP communication between Physical Twin and Digital Twin, ensuring real-time synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance SafetyEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 26262-compliant safety framework with watchdog timers and redundant command paths for fail-safe operation of ACC systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data acquisition, visualization, and reporting service for operators and developers. Provides UI for vehicle state, object tracks, and projected paths."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, DrivingPathInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Distributed computation service offloading ACC logic from on-board ECUs to cloud/edge infrastructure. Executes lead vehicle detection and actuation commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccLogicExecutionAction, ActuationCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Safety-critical remote control service for enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speed, and sending emergency braking commands with low-latency guarantees."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccControlAction, EmergencyBrakingAction, SystemConfigurationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle dynamics (speed, acceleration, braking status) and environmental conditions for operational monitoring."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of detected objects (lead vehicle, obstacles) with metadata (velocity, distance) for situational awareness."
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Display of projected driving path with trajectory points and safety margins derived from MPC optimization."
]

instance AccLogicExecutionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of ACC logic in the Digital Twin, including lead vehicle detection and desired speed computation based on distance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation and transmission of throttle/braking commands from the Digital Twin to the Physical Twin for execution."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AccControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enable/disable of ACC functionality and setting of desired speed parameters by operators."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Override command for emergency braking triggered by safety-critical events or operator intervention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SystemConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote configuration of ACC parameters, sensor calibration, and system settings for testing and development."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ThrottleActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor actuation system for throttle control, receiving commands from the Digital Twin for adaptive speed adjustments in ACC."
]

instance BrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Hydraulic/electric braking system for emergency braking and adaptive deceleration commands from the Digital Twin."
]

instance SteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo-controlled steering system for trajectory corrections derived from path planning in the Digital Twin."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the transmission of **control commands** from the **Digital Twin Entity** (or **User Entity**) to the **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., vehicle actuators) via the **Actuation FE** and **Controlling FE** components. This interaction is critical for adaptive control, such as **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** and dynamic path correction.

**Command Types**:
- **Motor Actuation Commands**: Adjust engine torque or braking force.
- **Steering Servo Commands**: Modify steering angle for path correction.
- **ACC Control Signals**: Regulate vehicle speed based on projected driving path and object tracks.

**Control Message Formats**:
- Standardized via the **Data Translation FE**, which ensures **message serialization** and **unit consistency** (e.g., converting digital commands to physical actuator units like degrees for steering or Nm for torque).
- Commands are formatted to align with **ROS2 message types** for intra-entity communication and **UDP packets** for inter-entity transmission.

**Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
- **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but implied through the **Data Translation FE**, which likely includes **checksum validation** or **message integrity checks** to prevent corrupted commands.
- **No explicit mention of cryptographic authentication**, but **UDP over Wi-Fi** suggests potential vulnerabilities requiring additional security layers (e.g., TLS or IPsec) in a real-world deployment.

**Feedback Systems**:
- **Closed-loop control** is implied via the **Controlling FE (ACC)**, which receives **real-time feedback** from the **Physical Twin Entity** (e.g., vehicle state updates, LiDAR object tracks) to adjust commands dynamically.
- **No explicit feedback confirmation mechanism** (e.g., acknowledgment packets) is described, but the **Actuation FE** likely includes **status monitoring** to detect command execution failures (e.g., actuator stalls).

**Conflict Resolution**:
- **Not explicitly described**, but the **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC) likely prioritizes **safety-critical commands** (e.g., emergency braking) over non-critical adjustments.
- **Arbitration logic** may exist within the **Actuation FE** to resolve conflicts between concurrent commands (e.g., steering vs. braking).

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- **No explicit confirmation protocol** is mentioned, but the **Data Translation FE** ensures **consistent message formatting**, which may include **execution status flags** (e.g., success/failure codes) in response messages.
- **Real-time monitoring** via the **3D Visualization** (Fig. 3a) allows operators to observe the effects of commands on the physical twin, indirectly confirming execution.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards but not fully achievable here. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services, but the **100 ms synchronization** serves as a baseline for real-time data exchange between sensors, actuators, and software components (e.g., ROS2 nodes). Latency requirements are implicitly tied to this synchronization, ensuring low-latency communication for time-sensitive operations like actuation and point cloud processing. No further granularity (e.g., microsecond-level precision) is documented beyond this constraint."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **entity-based orchestration pattern** rooted in ISO 23247 RA, where **software components (SWCs)** are mapped to **Functional Entities (FEs)** and deployed as **ROS2 packages** on heterogeneous hardware. Key coordination mechanisms include:
  - **ROS2 Middleware**: Enables decentralized execution of FEs (e.g., nodes for *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) with **inter-node communication** for real-time data flow.
  - **Hardware-Specific Deployment**: Each entity (*Physical Twin*, *Digital Twin*, *User*) runs on separate hardware, requiring **cross-entity synchronization** (e.g., 100 ms clock alignment) to maintain consistency.
  - **Deviations from ISO 23247**: The *Physical Twin Entity* consolidates both hardware-specific (sensors/actuators) and general automotive functionalities, unlike the standard’s *OME*. This impacts **ownership and engineering processes** by centralizing resource management.
  - **Resource Management**: Implicitly handled via ROS2’s **Quality of Service (QoS) policies** and hardware partitioning, though explicit details (e.g., load balancing) are absent. The system prioritizes **modularity** over centralized control, with FEs acting as autonomous yet coordinated units."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** of this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed for **real-time, low-latency communication** between the **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**. The architecture leverages a **hybrid network topology** combining **intra-entity (ROS2-based) and inter-entity (Wi-Fi/UDP) communication**.

**Communication Protocols**:
1. **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**:
   - **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)**: Used for **pub-sub messaging** between software components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**, **Presentation FE**) within the Digital Twin Entity. ROS2 provides **topic-based communication**, **service calls**, and **parameter servers** for modular interaction.
   - **Message Serialization**: ROS2 employs **ROS2 message types** (e.g., `sensor_msgs/PointCloud2`, `geometry_msgs/Twist`) for structured data exchange.

2. **Inter-Entity (Physical ↔ Digital Twin)**:
   - **Wi-Fi (IEEE 802.11)**: Used for **wireless transmission** of sensor data and control commands between the Physical Twin (e.g., vehicle sensors) and Digital Twin.
   - **UDP (User Datagram Protocol)**: Operates at the **transport layer** for **low-latency, connectionless communication**, prioritizing speed over reliability. This is critical for **real-time sensor data streaming** (e.g., LiDAR point clouds) and **control command delivery**.

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Star Topology**: The **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators) communicates directly with the **Digital Twin Entity** (central processing unit) via Wi-Fi/UDP.
- **ROS2 Network**: Within the Digital Twin Entity, components form a **distributed system** where nodes (e.g., **Data Collecting FE**, **Controlling FE**) exchange messages over a **local ROS2 network** (likely Ethernet or shared memory for high-speed communication).

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **LiDAR Data**: High bandwidth (≥100 Mbps) due to **point cloud density** (e.g., 64-channel LiDAR generating ~100k points/s). Latency must be **<50ms** for real-time clustering/tracking.
- **Vehicle State Data**: Moderate bandwidth (~10 Mbps), with latency **<10ms** for control loop stability.
- **Control Commands**: Low bandwidth (~1 Mbps), but **<10ms latency** is critical to avoid actuator lag.

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., failover nodes or backup Wi-Fi links) is mentioned, but **ROS2’s pub-sub model** allows for **multiple publishers** (e.g., redundant LiDAR sensors) to improve data robustness.
- **Data Translation FE** includes **unit conversion and message alignment**, which may implicitly handle **data loss or corruption** by re-syncing message streams.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Not explicitly detailed**, but risks include:
  - **UDP-based communication** is vulnerable to **packet loss or spoofing**.
  - **Wi-Fi transmission** may require **encryption (WPA3)** or **firewall rules** to prevent unauthorized access.
  - **ROS2 security extensions** (e.g., authentication via **ROS2 Security**) could be implemented for intra-entity protection.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **hosting and deployment infrastructure** of this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed for **real-time processing** with a focus on **edge computing** to minimize latency, though the document does not explicitly specify whether cloud or on-premise components are used. The system relies on **software components deployed across distributed nodes**, with **ROS2** as the primary framework for inter-component communication.

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Edge Deployment**: Likely dominates due to the **real-time constraints** of LiDAR processing, vehicle state monitoring, and control commands. The **Digital Twin Entity** (e.g., on-board computer or embedded system) hosts:
  - **Data Pre-Processing FE** (point cloud clustering/tracking).
  - **Controlling FE** (ACC logic).
  - **Presentation FE** (3D visualization).
  - **Data Translation FE** (message serialization/unit conversion).
- **No explicit mention of cloud hosting**, but **Data Storage FE** suggests **persistent storage** (e.g., local SSD or network-attached storage) for reporting and historical data.

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **CPU/GPU**: High-performance processing is required for:
  - **LiDAR point cloud processing** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or Intel Xeon for clustering/tracking).
  - **Real-time 3D visualization** (e.g., OpenGL/Vulkan rendering).
  - **Control algorithms** (e.g., PID controllers for ACC).
- **Memory**: **RAM ≥16GB** to handle concurrent ROS2 nodes and large point cloud datasets.
- **Storage**:
  - **Volatile**: Temporary buffers for sensor data streams (e.g., 1GB+ for LiDAR frames).
  - **Persistent**: Storage for **reports and historical data** (e.g., SQL/NoSQL database or file system).

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Modular ROS2 Components**: New sensors or control features can be added as **independent nodes**, scaling horizontally by deploying additional instances (e.g., redundant LiDAR processors).
- **Dynamic Topic Subscription**: ROS2’s **pub-sub model** allows components to scale by adjusting message rates or filtering data (e.g., downsampling LiDAR points).
- **No explicit cloud scaling** (e.g., Kubernetes orchestration) is mentioned, but **edge devices** could be clustered for distributed processing.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **On-Premise/Edge**: Primary model, with all critical components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**) running locally on the vehicle’s embedded system.
- **Hybrid Possible**: If **Data Storage FE** or **Reporting FE** offloads data to a **central server**, a hybrid edge-cloud model could emerge, though not explicitly described.

**Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
- **Real-Time Constraints**: The system prioritizes **deterministic latency** for control loops (e.g., ACC), implying **hardware/software co-design** to meet deadlines.
- **Fault Tolerance**:
  - **ROS2’s node recovery** mechanisms can restart failed components.
  - **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., hot standby) is mentioned, but **multiple sensor inputs** (e.g., redundant LiDAR) improve robustness.
  - **Data Translation FE** ensures **message consistency**, reducing errors from corrupted streams.
- **Monitoring**: **3D Visualization** and **Reporting FE** provide **real-time and historical insights** into system health, enabling proactive maintenance.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard (2021)**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - **Entity-Based Framework**: A modular architecture comprising **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**, each with well-defined responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: While not explicitly named, the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** suggests compliance with **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for **machine-to-machine communication** and **data exchange** between physical and digital layers.
  - **Certification Requirements**: The standard implies alignment with **manufacturing system standards** (e.g., **IEC 62264** for enterprise-control system integration) but does not mandate specific certifications. **Compliance frameworks** are inferred to include **ISO 9001** (quality management) and **ISO/IEC 27001** (information security) for enterprise-grade DT deployments.
  - **Information Exchange**: Part 4 of ISO 23247 (not detailed here) likely addresses **semantic interoperability** (e.g., **ontology-based modeling** for manufacturing elements), though no specific ontology (e.g., **STEP, GAIA**) is referenced in the provided context.

  The architecture is **technology-agnostic** but implicitly supports **cross-vendor interoperability** through standardized entity interfaces."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for **functional safety** in road vehicles, which specifies **automotive safety integrity levels (ASILs)** and **safety mechanisms** to mitigate risks in safety-critical systems. This includes:
  - **Cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **ISO/SAE 21434** (though not explicitly mentioned, implied by ISO 26262-1’s emphasis on security in safety-critical systems) and **3GPP TS 22.185** for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services**, which mandates **authentication, authorization, and encryption** for secure communication between vehicles and infrastructure.
  - **Access control mechanisms** tied to **role-based access** and **safety-critical system segmentation** to prevent unauthorized modifications, as required by **ISO 26262-1** for **safety-related software**.
  - **Fail-safe mechanisms** such as **watchdog timers, redundant system checks, and automatic fallback modes** to ensure system stability in case of failures, per **ISO 26262-1’s functional safety requirements**.
  - **Risk assessment approaches** following **ISO 26262-1’s hazard analysis and risk assessment (HARA)** methodology, which classifies risks into **ASIL levels (A-D)** and defines mitigation strategies.
  - **Threat mitigation strategies** for **cyber-physical attacks**, including **secure boot processes, intrusion detection systems (IDS), and cryptographic protection** of communication channels, as implied by **3GPP TS 22.185’s security requirements for V2X services**.
  - **Safety-critical system considerations** in **model-driven architecture (MDA)** for Digital Twin cockpits (as per **Dalibor et al., 2020**), where **formal verification methods** (e.g., **UML/SysML models**) ensure compliance with **ISO 26262-1’s safety lifecycle processes**. The system must also support **real-time monitoring** of safety-critical parameters to detect deviations and trigger corrective actions."
]
